name: ai-evidence-panel
title: AI Evidence Panel
description: Collapsible provenance section grouping citations with a retrieval date and caller-authored limitations.

props.name: retrievedAt
props.description: When the sources were retrieved. Evidence ages; say when it was pulled.

props.name: limitations
props.description: What the evidence does not establish, including sources that could not be read.

props.name: count
props.description: Override the count in the header. Defaults to the number of children.

clinicalNotes: Collapsed by default so an answer stays readable, but the count and retrieval date are visible without expanding — a clinician can see at a glance that an answer rests on two sources from six weeks ago. Put the sources you could NOT read in the limitations, not in silence: an evidence panel that lists three accessible sources and omits the inaccessible pharmacy record implies a completeness the answer does not have. This is the audit trail a reviewer works from, so never populate it with sources the model did not actually use.
notes: Depends on ai-citation. Collapsible animation classes come from tw-animate-css.
